errorPattern: Can't call $ on element with selector "[role="tab"][aria-label="Video transcript"]" because element wasn't found

fix.summary: Production renders the transcript tab aria-label as "Video transcripts" (plural); some builds use the singular form. Use a starts-with match so both work.
fix.pattern: Change the transcript tab selector from [role="tab"][aria-label="Video transcript"] to [role="tab"][aria-label^="Video transcript"]. Same pattern is already centralized as transcriptSelectors.tab in AdeptVideoPlayerPage.

investigationFindings: Git commit 01e086f (PR #60) confirms production DOM uses the plural label; verified via live inspection. The page object now centralizes the selector with [aria-label^="Video transcript"] for both desktop and mobile transcript flows.
rootCauseChain: SELECTOR_MISMATCH -> Environment-specific aria-label -> exact match fails -> waitForExist/click fails
